summary: O'Neil shares a photograph from Haiti of a young boy pointing to the sky, using it as a pointed response to Trump's reported 'shithole countries' comments about Haiti and African nations. The brief post serves as both a tribute to Haiti's dignity and a rebuke of presidential disrespect. She frames the child's gesture as a reminder that moral authority transcends political power.
key_points: A chance encounter in Haiti three years prior becomes politically resonant in light of Trump's comments
The child's spontaneous gesture of pointing to God carries symbolic weight against presidential disrespect
Moral and spiritual authority is positioned as superior to political power
money_quotes: There is a power higher than that of any president.
key_insight: The simple faith of a Haitian child serves as a quiet but powerful rebuke to political leaders who demean entire nations.
